sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: 3 entities with hashes: b@671d83f (39,48), w@09cf769 (63,37), B@7af8c7c (49,16). Cursor at (60,27). Targets: 3 red, 2 blue, 3 orange. Entities move autonomously. Need to understand movement pattern.
Goal model: Align all entities on matching targets simultaneously.
Plan: Switch to orange X, track its position, then try positioning on target.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me track entity positions across switches to understand movement
# First, record current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Get all entity info
entities = {}
for n in nodes:
    if n['pixels'] > 20:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        center = (sum(rows)//len(rows), sum(cols)//len(cols))
        entities[n['hash']] = {'color': n['color'], 'center': center, 'pixels': n['pixels']}

print("Current entities:")
for h, e in entities.items():
    print(f"  hash={h[:8]}: color={e['color']}, center={e['center']}, pixels={e['pixels']}")

# Find cursor
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] == 1:
        cursor = n['boundary'][0]
        print(f"\nCursor: {cursor}")

# Now switch to orange X and record positions
action(['SPACE'])

seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
nodes2 = seg2['nodes']

entities2 = {}
for n in nodes2:
    if n['pixels'] > 20:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        center = (sum(rows)//len(rows), sum(cols)//len(cols))
        entities2[n['hash']] = {'color': n['color'], 'center': center, 'pixels': n['pixels']}

print("\nAfter SPACE:")
for h, e in entities2.items():
    print(f"  hash={h[:8]}: color={e['color']}, center={e['center']}, pixels={e['pixels']}")

for n in nodes2:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] == 1:
        print(f"Cursor: {n['boundary'][0]}")

# Check targets
print("\nTargets:")
for n in nodes2:
    if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] == 8:
        for cid in n['children']:
            cn = nodes2[cid]
            print(f"  {cn['color']}@{cn['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
